title: Migration module: reset endpoint, blog-category detection, additional images, bleach sanitization
description:
Four targeted fixes to the OpenCart migration module. Work in this exact order:

## ANALYSIS FINDINGS (pre-read by orchestrator)
- `backend/app/api/v1/admin/migration_service.py` — migrate_catalog() exists, no blog-category detection, no additional images, description_html stores raw HTML (no bleach strip)
- `backend/app/api/v1/admin/router.py` — has start/pause/resume migration endpoints. NO reset endpoint.
- `backend/app/db/opencart_models.py` — OCProductImage model is MISSING
- `backend/app/db/models/blog.py` — BlogPost model exists; fields: id, title, slug, content_html, content_json, status (BlogPostStatus), cover_image, published_at, author_id (NOT NULL FK to blog_authors). No oc_product_id field.
- `backend/app/db/models/product.py` — Product has oc_product_id field already.

## Fix 1 — Reset endpoint
Add `DELETE /api/v1/admin/migration/reset` in `backend/app/api/v1/admin/router.py`.

Logic (in MigrationService.reset_migration()):
1. Delete all OrderItem rows (CASCADE or explicit)
2. Delete all Order rows where oc_order_id IS NOT NULL
3. Delete all ProductImage rows for products with oc_product_id IS NOT NULL
4. Delete all ProductVariant rows for products with oc_product_id IS NOT NULL
5. Delete all Product rows where oc_product_id IS NOT NULL
6. Delete all Category rows where oc_category_id IS NOT NULL (use recursive delete respecting FK, or set parent_id=NULL first)
7. Delete all BlogPost rows where oc_product_id IS NOT NULL (after Fix 3 adds that field)
8. Delete all MigrationJob rows (all of them)
9. Commit
Return: `{"status": "ok", "message": "Migration data cleared"}`
Auth: require_admin dependency.

## Fix 2 — OCProductImage model
Add to `backend/app/db/opencart_models.py`:
```python
class OCProductImage(OCBase):
    __tablename__ = "oc_product_image"
    product_image_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    product_id: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer)
    image: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(255))
    sort_order: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, default=0)
```
Import it in migration_service.py.

## Fix 3 — BlogPost.oc_product_id field + Alembic migration
Add to BlogPost model in `backend/app/db/models/blog.py`:
```python
oc_product_id: Mapped[Optional[int]] = mapped_column(Integer, index=True, nullable=True)
```
Create Alembic migration: `alembic revision --autogenerate -m "blog: add oc_product_id to blog_posts"`
Review and apply.

## Fix 4 — migrate_catalog() improvements
In `backend/app/api/v1/admin/migration_service.py`, rewrite migrate_catalog():

### 4a — Blog-category detection
Before product loop, build a set of `blog_category_oc_ids: set[int]` by checking category names.
Blog keywords (case-insensitive): "новости", "статьи", "инструкции", "blog", "news", "articles", "статья", "новость".
For each OCCategoryDescription where name.lower() contains any keyword, add its category_id to the set.
Also collect all oc_category_ids that map to these blog categories from OCProductToCategory.

### 4b — Product vs BlogPost routing
For each oc_prod in the batch:
- Get all category links via OCProductToCategory
- If ANY of the linked oc_category_ids is in blog_category_oc_ids → create BlogPost
- Otherwise → create Product (existing logic)

BlogPost creation:
- Check idempotency: `select(BlogPost).where(BlogPost.oc_product_id == oc_prod.product_id)` — skip if exists
- title = oc_desc.name (or f"Post {oc_prod.product_id}")
- slug = self._slugify(title), ensure unique via _ensure_unique_slug(BlogPost, slug)
- content_html = oc_desc.description or "" (raw HTML — stored as-is in content_html)
- content_json = {} (empty dict, required field)
- status = BlogPostStatus.PUBLISHED if oc_prod.status else BlogPostStatus.DRAFT
- published_at = oc_prod.date_added.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) if oc_prod.date_added else datetime.now(timezone.utc)
- oc_product_id = oc_prod.product_id
- IMPORTANT: author_id is NOT NULL in BlogPost. You must find or create a system author. Query `select(Author).limit(1)` — use first author. If no authors exist, create one linked to first admin user: `select(User).where(User.role == 'admin').limit(1)`. If no admin either, skip BlogPost creation and log a warning.
- cover_image: download oc_prod.image via _download_image() if present
- Tags: parse oc_desc.meta_keyword (split by comma and space, strip), add tag "импорт". For each tag: get_or_create Tag(name=tag, slug=slugify(tag)), append to post.tags

### 4c — Additional images for products
After creating new_prod and its cover image, fetch OCProductImage rows:
```python
oc_additional_imgs = select(OCProductImage).where(OCProductImage.product_id == oc_prod.product_id).order_by(OCProductImage.sort_order)
```
For each row: download image, create ProductImage(product=new_prod, url=local_path, is_cover=False, alt=name)

### 4d — description bleach sanitization for Products
For Product creation, change:
- description_html = oc_desc.description (keep raw HTML)
- description = bleach.clean(oc_desc.description, tags=[], strip=True) if oc_desc.description else None
(plain text for search/preview)
Add `import bleach` at top. bleach should already be in requirements.txt; if not, add it.

### 4e — Meilisearch sync after product creation
After self.session.commit() at end of batch, trigger Meilisearch indexing:
```python
try:
    from app.tasks.search_index import sync_products_to_meilisearch  # noqa: PLC0415
    sync_products_to_meilisearch.delay()
except Exception:
    pass  # Non-critical
```

## Files to modify
- `backend/app/db/opencart_models.py` — add OCProductImage
- `backend/app/db/models/blog.py` — add oc_product_id field
- `backend/app/api/v1/admin/migration_service.py` — all logic changes
- `backend/app/api/v1/admin/router.py` — add reset endpoint
- `backend/migrations/versions/` — new migration file

## Requirements check
- `bleach` must be in `backend/requirements.txt` (add if missing)
- `import bleach` in migration_service.py
- All new code: ruff + mypy clean
- No Any types in new code
- All admin endpoints: require_admin
- Do NOT touch CDEK integration
acceptance_criteria: DELETE /api/v1/admin/migration/reset endpoint exists and requires admin role
reset_migration() deletes products, categories, orders, blog posts (oc_product_id IS NOT NULL) and all MigrationJob rows
BlogPost.oc_product_id field added with Alembic migration
migrate_catalog() detects blog categories by name keywords and routes products to BlogPost
BlogPost creation handles missing author gracefully (log warning, skip)
Product.description is bleach-stripped plain text; description_html keeps raw HTML
Additional product images (oc_product_image) downloaded and saved as ProductImage(is_cover=False)
Meilisearch sync triggered after each catalog batch commit
ruff check passes with no errors
alembic check passes (no pending model changes without migration)
